heading: Delete an API (Application Programming Interface) Access List Entry for the API (Application Programming Interface) Key
body:
To delete an IP access list entry for your API key using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists delete <entry> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-apiKeys-accessLists-delete).

- Click  to the right of the API (Application Programming Interface) Key.

- Click Edit Permissions.

> **Note:** Selecting Edit Permissions takes you to the
> organization level of the Atlas console.

You cannot modify an existing API (Application Programming Interface) Key access list entry.
You must delete and re-create it.

- Click  to the right of the IP (Internet Protocol) address to
  remove it.

- Add the new IP (Internet Protocol) address or CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block from which you want
  Atlas to accept API (Application Programming Interface) requests for this API (Application Programming Interface) Key. Use one of
  the two options:

  - Click Add access list Entry and type an IP (Internet Protocol) address, or

  - Click Use Current IP Address if the host
    you are using to access Atlas will also make API (Application Programming Interface) requests
    using this API (Application Programming Interface) Key.

- Click Save.
